content.text:
```json
{
  "module": "scheduling",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 22,
    "domain_depth": 28,
    "data_richness": 15,
    "interaction_quality": 18,
    "ai_integration": 5,
    "mobile_first": 12,
    "performance": 25,
    "typescript_quality": 35,
    "error_handling": 20,
    "realtime": 18,
    "accessibility": 10,
    "security": 22,
    "differentiation": 8,
    "enterprise_readiness": 15
  },
  "overall_score": 18,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "scheduling-C7-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "domain_depth",
      "title": "No interactive Gantt chart component exists — schedule page lacks core construction visualization",
      "file": "src/pages/Schedule.tsx",
      "description": "The Schedule page has no Gantt chart rendering. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA requires interactive Gantt with phase bars, dependency arrows, critical path highlighting, milestones, baseline comparison, and zoom levels. Currently the page likely shows a table or placeholder. Without Gantt, the schedule module is non-functional for any superintendent or PM.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Schedule.tsx, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The page renders schedule data as a flat table or list with no Gantt visualization, no dependency arrows, no critical path highlighting, no baseline comparison, and no zoom controls. AFTER: Replace the main content area with a full interactive Gantt chart. Implementation: (1) Add a GanttChart section below the metric cards. Each schedule_phase/activity renders as a horizontal bar positioned by start_date/end_date relative to a time axis. (2) Color bars by status: not_started=#9CA3AF, in_progress=#3B82F6, completed=#4EC896, delayed=#E74C3C. (3) Highlight critical path activities (is_critical_path===true) with a red left border (3px solid #E74C3C). (4) Show baseline dates as ghost bars (gray, 50% opacity) behind actual bars when baseline_start/baseline_end exist. (5) Add zoom controls: day/week/month buttons that adjust the time axis scale. (6) Each bar shows name on hover with a tooltip containing: name, start_date, end_date, percent_complete%, float_days days float. (7) Render a percent_complete fill inside each bar as a darker shade. (8) Milestones (is_milestone===true or duration===0) render as diamond markers instead of bars. (9) Add a timeline header row showing date labels. (10) Use inline styles with theme tokens. The Gantt container should have overflow-x:auto for horizontal scrolling. Bars use position:absolute inside a relative container, with left and width calculated as percentages of the total project duration. Use the existing useScheduleActivities or equivalent hook for data. If the data array is empty, show the empty state: 'Build your schedule to track every phase from mobilization to closeout.' with a 'Create First Phase' CTA button and an 'Import from P6/MS Project' secondary button. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "scheduling-C7-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "data_richness",
      "title": "Schedule page has no metric cards — missing KPI bar for schedule health",
      "file": "src/pages/Schedule.tsx",
      "description": "PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Section 6 requires KPI Cards: Schedule Variance (days), Critical Path Items, On Track %, Complete %. The page currently lacks these summary metrics. Every page must start with 3-5 metric cards per design standards.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Schedule.tsx, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The page has no metric summary cards at the top showing schedule health KPIs. AFTER: Add a row of 4 metric cards at the top of the page, above the Gantt/table content. Calculate these from the schedule data array: (1) 'Schedule Variance' — compute as the difference between the latest activity's planned end_date and its projected end (use actual_finish if completed, else end_date). Show as '+X days' (green, ahead) or '-X days' (red, behind) or 'On Track' (green). Icon: Calendar. (2) 'Critical Path Items' — count of activities where is_critical_path===true. Color: red if >0, gray if 0. Icon: AlertTriangle. (3) 'On Track' — percentage of activities where status is not 'delayed'. Format as 'XX%'. Green if >=80%, amber if 50-79%, red if <50%. Icon: TrendingUp. (4) 'Complete' — average of all percent_complete values across activities. Format as 'XX%'. Show a small progress ring or bar. Icon: CheckCircle. Use the MetricBox or Card primitive from src/components/Primitives.tsx. Layout: display:grid, gridTemplateColumns:'repeat(4, 1fr)', gap:theme.spacing.md. Each card should be clickable (future: filter Gantt to relevant subset). When data is loading, show 4 skeleton cards matching the card dimensions. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "scheduling-C7-003",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "interaction_quality",
      "title": "No schedule import functionality — cannot upload P6 XER or MS Project files",
      "file": "src/pages/Schedule.tsx",
      "description": "PRODUCTION_ROADMAP P0-2 requires schedule import from .xer, .xml, and .mpp files. The empty state per acceptance criteria must include an 'Import from P6/MS Project' CTA. Currently no import UI or file upload exists. This is a P0 dealbreaker — supers cannot use the schedule without importing from their existing P6 system.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Schedule.tsx, make these specific changes: BEFORE: There is no 'Import Schedule' button or file upload dialog anywhere on the schedule page. Users cannot bring in their Primavera P6 or MS Project schedules. AFTER: Add an 'Import Schedule' button in the page header area (next to any existing 'Create Phase' button). The button should have a Calendar+Upload icon and text 'Import Schedule'. On click, open a modal dialog with: (1) Title: 'Import Schedule'. (2) Drag-and-drop zone with text 'Drop your schedule file here or click to browse'. Accept file types: .xer, .xml, .mpp. (3) File type detection: show the detected format after selection ('Primavera P6 XER detected' or 'MS Project detected' or 'P6 XML detected'). (4) After file selection, show a 'Preview & Import' button. (5) For now, since the edge function does not exist yet, clicking 'Preview & Import' should show a toast: 'Schedule import is being configured. Your file has been queued for processing.' and close the modal. Store the file reference in component state. (6) Also add an 'Import from P6/MS Project' link/button in the empty state. (7) The modal should have a close X button and Cancel button. Use inline styles consistent with other modals in the app (white background, border-radius 12px, padding 24px, overlay with rgba(0,0,0,0.5)). After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "scheduling-C7-004",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "accessibility",
      "title": "Schedule page lacks ARIA attributes, keyboard navigation, and screen reader support",
      "file": "src/pages/Schedule.tsx",
      "description": "Global acceptance criteria require all pages pass axe-core with zero critical/serious violations, keyboard navigable tab order, focus rings, and aria-labels on all interactive elements. The schedule page has no ARIA roles on the Gantt region, no keyboard controls for zoom or navigation, and no aria-live announcements for status changes.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Schedule.tsx, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The schedule page has no ARIA attributes on the main content area, metric cards lack aria-label, interactive buttons lack aria-label, the Gantt/table region has no role='region' or aria-label, and there is no keyboard navigation support for zoom or activity selection. AFTER: (1) Add role='main' and aria-label='Project Schedule' to the outermost container div. (2) Add aria-label to each metric card describing its value (e.g., aria-label='Schedule Variance: 3 days behind'). (3) Add role='region' and aria-label='Schedule Gantt Chart' to the Gantt container. (4) Add aria-label to the 'Import Schedule' button: aria-label='Import schedule from Primavera P6 or Microsoft Project'. (5) Add aria-label to zoom control buttons: aria-label='Zoom to day view', aria-label='Zoom to week view', aria-label='Zoom to month view'. (6) Add tabIndex={0} and onKeyDown handlers to activity bars in the Gantt so users can press Enter to view details and arrow keys to navigate between activities. (7) Add an aria-live='polite' region that announces when data loads: 'Schedule loaded with X activities, Y on critical path'. (8) Ensure all status filter buttons/tabs have aria-pressed or aria-selected attributes. (9) Add a visually-hidden heading h1 'Schedule' for screen reader landmark navigation. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "scheduling-C7-005",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "mobile_first",
      "title": "Schedule page not usable on mobile — no responsive layout or touch-friendly timeline",
      "file": "src/pages/Schedule.tsx",
      "description": "PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Section 6 requires: 'Mobile: Gantt replaced with a timeline list view (vertical). Each phase is a card with start/end dates, progress bar, and status. Tap to expand details.' Currently the page likely renders a desktop-only layout that overflows or becomes unusable at 375px width.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Schedule.tsx, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The schedule page renders identically on mobile and desktop with no responsive breakpoints, causing horizontal overflow, tiny touch targets, and an unusable Gantt chart on phone screens. AFTER: (1) Detect screen width using a useState+useEffect that listens to window resize (or use matchMedia for 768px breakpoint). (2) On mobile (width < 768px): hide the horizontal Gantt chart entirely. Instead, render a vertical timeline list where each activity is a card containing: activity name (bold, 14px), date range ('Mar 15 - Apr 2'), a horizontal progress bar (height 6px, green fill to percent_complete%), status badge (colored dot + text), and float days if > 0. (3) Cards should have min-height 64px with padding 16px, border-radius 8px, margin-bottom 8px. Critical path cards get a red left border (3px). (4) Metric cards at top: stack to a 2x2 grid on mobile (gridTemplateColumns: 'repeat(2, 1fr)'). (5) Add a filter bar above the list: horizontal scroll of pill buttons for status filter (All, In Progress, Delayed, Critical Path). (6) Each card is tappable — add onClick that could open a detail panel (for now, show a console.log or a toast with the activity name). (7) Touch targets: ensure all tappable areas are at least 44x44px. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [
    {
      "title": "Weather-Aware Schedule Risk Overlay",
      "description": "Overlay 14-day weather forecast on the Gantt chart timeline. Activities flagged as outdoor_activity that coincide with rain/snow/extreme heat days get amber warning icons. AI calculates cumulative weather delay risk and suggests rescheduling. 'Rain forecasted Tuesday through Thursday — concrete pour on Phase 3 at risk. Recommend reschedule to Friday.' This is a Tier 3 vision feature that no competitor ships today.",
      "technical_approach": "Use existing weather_cache table data. Add outdoor_activity boolean column to schedule_phases (already in P0-2 mapping). Edge function ai-schedule-risk cross-references weather forecast with outdoor activities. Frontend renders weather icons on the Gantt timeline header and warning badges on affected activity bars.",
      "competitive_advantage": "Procore has no weather-schedule integration. ACC has Construction IQ but it does not predict weather delays at the activity level. Fieldwire has no schedule at all. This would be a demo-winning feature."
    }
  ],
  "competitive_intel": "Procore's scheduling is basic — no inline Gantt editing, no critical path calculation, no weather overlay. Most GCs export from P6 to PDF and pin it on the wall. ACC has better schedule visualization through Navisworks but requires BIM. Fieldwire has zero scheduling capability. SiteSync's opportunity is a lightweight, field-first schedule viewer that imports from P6 and adds AI weather risk — something nobody ships today. The current implementation is far from this vision: no Gantt, no import, no critical path highlighting, no mobile timeline. The gap to competitive parity is large but achievable with P0-2 (import) and the Gantt chart component.",
  "summary": "The Scheduling module scores 18/100 — the lowest-scoring module in the app. The fundamental problem is the absence of a Gantt chart, which is the core visualization every construction PM expects from a schedule page. Without it, the page is a data table that adds no value over a spreadsheet. The five issues target: (1) building the Gantt chart with critical path, baselines, and milestones, (2) adding KPI metric cards for schedule health, (3) implementing the P0-2 schedule import flow for P6/MS Project files, (4) adding comprehensive ARIA/keyboard accessibility, and (5) making the page mobile-usable with a vertical timeline card layout. These changes would take the module from non-functional to genuinely useful for a superintendent checking the schedule at 6 AM on their phone. The weather-aware schedule risk overlay is the invented feature that would differentiate SiteSync from every competitor in the market."
}
```
